description_long: This object is a classic rectangular picnic table with two long benches attached on either side, supported by sturdy A-frame legs. The tabletop is flat and wide, providing ample space for dining or gathering, while the benches run parallel to the length of the table and are securely fixed to the frame. The structure is reinforced with diagonal braces connecting the legs to the underside of the tabletop and benches, ensuring stability and durability. The silhouette suggests the table is constructed primarily from thick wooden planks, typical of outdoor furniture found in parks or recreational areas. The design is robust and functional, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on practicality and communal use. There is no visible hole in the center of the tabletop for an umbrella.
description: A sturdy rectangular picnic table with attached benches and A-frame supports, constructed from thick wooden planks. Designed for communal outdoor seating and dining.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the table and benches, with the A-frame legs and cross-bracing visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the width of the tabletop and benches, as well as the angled support structure beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the opposite side of the table and benches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the width and the A-frame support structure.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by two A-frame legs on each end, connected by diagonal braces and crossbeams that provide stability and distribute weight evenly.
